commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cf77fcbb658621628215d02d64e87c5e448a1dc5)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds transient Dashboard webchat reply state, a custom right-click Reply menu, a composer preview, quoted Markdown send prepending, and related Control UI styles.

PR surface: Source +221. Total +221 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defects: exact-head source shows the production caller omits the required reply props while the handler suppresses native right-click before returning, and the CSS regression is visible by comparing the PR head with current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed to fix the blocking UI issues and provide refreshed exact-head proof; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair while the real-behavior-proof gate is insufficient.

target.title: feat(control-ui): add right-click Reply in Dashboard webchat
target.body:
## Summary - Adds right-click Reply to messages in the Dashboard / Control UI webchat - Right-click any chat bubble → "Reply" option → reply preview bar appears above the composer → type reply → message auto-prepended with Markdown blockquote - Click ✕ on the preview bar or press Escape to cancel the reply ### How it works Three new optional props on `ChatProps` (`replyTarget`, `onSetReply`, `onClearReply`) wired through `app-render.ts` into `renderChat`. A `@state() chatReplyTarget` on the app class holds the transient reply target. On send, `handleSendChat` reads the target, prepends a Markdown blockquote via `prependReplyQuote`, and clears the state. The preview bar renders above the composer while a target is active. ### Quote format (what the model receives) | Quoted message | Your reply | Sent to model | |---|---|---| | `hello world` | `got it` | `> **User:** hello world`

`got it` | | `line one`
`line two` | `ok` | `> **User:**`
`> line one`
`> line two`

`ok` | ## Linked context Closes #16896 ## Real behavior proof - **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw dev build (vite), WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 on Windows 11, Chromium - **Exact steps:** 1. `cd ui && pnpm dev` 2. Right-click a chat bubble → context menu shows speech-bubble icon + "Reply" 3. Click "Reply" → preview bar appears above composer with sender name, quoted text, and visible ✕ 4. Type a response and send 5. Sent message includes `> **User:** ...` Markdown blockquote (single or multi-line, no double `>`) 6. Click ✕ or press Escape → preview bar dismissed, plain send unaffected - **Evidence:** ## Files changed ``` ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts — prependReplyQuote + queue ordering fix ui/src/ui/app-render.ts — wire replyTarget/onSetReply/onClearReply into renderChat ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts — add chatReplyTarget to AppViewState type ui/src/ui/app.ts — add @state() chatReplyTarget to OpenClawApp ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts — context-menu handler, reply preview bar, ChatProps additions ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css — reply preview bar + context menu styles ``` ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change:** Yes — new right-click Reply in Control UI - **Config / migration / security / network:** No changes - **Highest-risk area:** `bubble.textContent` extraction for quote text; mitigated by `.trim().slice(0, 500)` - **Risk mitigation:** Reply target is transient state only, cleared on send or dismiss. No persistence.
target.author: programmingwtf

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
